name: Dylan Teugels
sub: 5+ years delivering performant webshops for enterprise merchants. I integrate Adobe Commerce with ERP, CRM, PIM and middleware — architecting clean boundaries so the platform stays fast and every system stays in sync.

timeline.desc: Joined PHPro as Hyvä was freshly launched. Picked it up early and got hands-on with it while the ecosystem was still forming.
Grew into increasingly complex work: integrations and the delicate parts of the codebase. Picked up Scrum Master as an extra role for the team.
Client discovery, functional analysis, architecture decisions, technical analysis, preparing dev-ready work.

capabilities.title: Enterprise Integrations via Middleware
capabilities.teaser: Wiring Adobe Commerce to SAP, Salesforce and other enterprise systems.
capabilities.what: On the Adobe Commerce side, I integrate the platform with ERP, CRM, PIM and other enterprise systems — via REST and SOAP APIs, message queues, or middleware/iPaaS, depending on what the architecture calls for. Customer, product, pricing and order flows all live here.
capabilities.why: Integration is where enterprise commerce lives or dies. Picking the right pattern per flow — direct API, queued, or middleware-routed — keeps Adobe Commerce clean and the flows reliable.

capabilities.title: Customer Master Data Architecture
capabilities.teaser: Salesforce as the source of truth for customer identity.
capabilities.what: Designing where customer identity lives — typically a CRM as the master — and how IDs, VAT numbers, and account states sync between Adobe Commerce, ERP and CRM. Covers first-order ID assignment, lead-vs-account logic, and customer data sync strategy.
capabilities.why: One source of truth for customer identity across the commerce stack means no duplicates, no drift. Get it wrong and every downstream flow — orders, pricing, invoicing — drifts with it.

capabilities.title: Product Data & PIM
capabilities.teaser: Akeneo PIM flowing cleanly from product data to storefront.
capabilities.what: Integrating a PIM with Adobe Commerce on the commerce side — product model design, attribute mapping, taxonomy, and making sure simple products stay simple instead of being forced into complex structures. Works with Akeneo or any equivalent PIM.
capabilities.why: Correct product modeling from PIM → Commerce is foundational. Get it wrong and you pollute search, pricing and checkout downstream.

capabilities.title: Search at Scale
capabilities.teaser: Algolia replacing Magento search at enterprise catalog size.
capabilities.what: Integrating a dedicated search engine with Adobe Commerce — attribute-driven discovery, faceted filtering, autocomplete, and relevance tuning. Algolia, Elasticsearch or equivalent.
capabilities.why: At enterprise catalog size, default Magento search doesn't scale. A dedicated search layer delivers faceted results at latency merchants actually notice.

capabilities.title: Hyvä Full-Stack Frontend
capabilities.teaser: Hyvä early adopter since 2021; Checkout customized at module level.
capabilities.what: Full Hyvä and Hyvä Enterprise storefronts. Hyvä Checkout customized at module level to match client-specific business rules, payment flows and B2B requirements.
capabilities.why: Checkout is the highest-risk, highest-value surface in commerce. Module-level Hyvä Checkout work requires deep knowledge of both Hyvä and Magento internals — not theme tweaks, real extension of the checkout module.

capabilities.title: B2B Commerce Complexity
capabilities.teaser: Company admin, shared address books, role-based pricing, quick order.
capabilities.what: Full Adobe Commerce B2B surface — company admin with shared address books and role-based permissions, negotiable pricing and quotes, approval workflows, shared catalogs, requisition and quick-order flows — the full feature set.
capabilities.why: B2B commerce on Adobe Commerce is its own specialty — merchant org structure, permission models and price list management are where most implementations fail.

capabilities.title: Multi-Store / Multi-Currency / International
capabilities.teaser: One installation, many markets — multi-currency, GeoIP redirect.
capabilities.what: International storefront architecture: multi-currency, multi-country, GeoIP-driven routing — one installation, many markets.
capabilities.why: Scaling to new countries without duplicating the platform is the whole point. Done right, each new market is configuration, not a fork.

capabilities.title: Technical Leadership
capabilities.teaser: Architecture, client strategy, project charters — the bridge role.
capabilities.what: Client discovery, functional analysis, architecture decisions, technical analysis, preparing dev-ready work, team meetings, steering committee attendance.
capabilities.why: The bridge between business intent and engineering execution. Being named on project charters alongside PM and PO is formal recognition of that role.
